channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: “We believe that he can be the difference-maker for us” – NiKo on playing with karrigan in the Lanxess
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to legendary Falcons rifler, Nikola ‘NiKo’ Kovač, before Team Falcons’ IEM Cologne 2026 Major quarter-final against Team Vitality.

The veteran star reflected on Falcons’ victory over NAVI, the team’s growing understanding under Finn ‘karrigan’ Andersen, and his own new responsibilities in the roster.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Insider Gaming interview with Niko at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major

You’re in the quarter-finals. You have some good memories in the Lanxess, but some bad memories as well. How are you feeling going into the playoffs?

Feeling very good, really happy with the way we won versus NAVI. Super excited to be back in Lanxess again. It’s a special place for CS and the community as well.

Now, this time around, it’s also the Major, so it’s going to be even more special to play in the arena. Just looking forward to the game.

So you mentioned there, you were pleased with how you beat NAVI. What aspects of that win were meaningful to you?

I would say that we had a very strong mental state against them. We were down on Anubis in the second half, 11-6 or 11-7. We managed to come back, you could even argue that we could have won without overtime, but we are still really happy with the way we approached overtime, and we managed to close it out.

Everyone had his own moments. You know, TeSeS has been on fire versus NAVI. It was really nice to see him playing the way he did.

He just showed up on the day, and it seemed that with the opponents getting better, we also stepped up our game a little bit as well, so that was very important to see.

And yeah, beating Na’Vi in such a close fashion was a good confidence boost for the playoffs.

It feels like the T-sides are becoming more online. How are you feeling in the server? Is there more connective tissue now between the players on the T-side?

Yeah, I mean, Finn is a very special IGL and one of the most experienced players out there with a really good track record as well. I think what he brings the most to the T-side is versatility, I would say.

We are very, very versatile on the T-side. The rounds can be very open or can also be very structured. So we have, I think, a pretty good balance of when we need to play together as a team and when some players need to do something individually as well.

I think he’s obviously still learning about the players on the team because he has just joined one month ago, basically. But so far, it has been very good working with him. He brings a lot of new ideas, just a different style, a different identity to the team as well.

It’s been nice working with him so far.

  • Related: Vitality’s IGL apEX interviewed at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major

Last time you played with karrigan, he didn’t have that reputation of having an arena buff. Now that the roster is feeling more cohesive, do you think that experience is going to be valuable when it comes to those big arena moments?

Definitely. That’s definitely one of the reasons why we decided to bring such an experience. The player has already won a lot of stuff. We believe that he can be the thing that can take us to the next level.

You know, Finn is always a special player when it comes to the stage matches. He’s a showman, and he brings something special out of the players and also the crowd.

So, this is going to be the first time that we share like a really big stage together, you know, even though we have played in Astana and Shanghai. But this time around, the Lanxess is very special.

It’s going to be nice to be on the stage with him, and we believe that he can be the difference-maker for us when it comes to these games.

We know you’ve been calling CT-sides for a while. In terms of the T-side, would you give that kind of secondary voice in the mid-round?

Yeah. As you mentioned, I’ve been already calling the CT-side, even though the CT side is kind of a shared responsibility. Everyone has to contribute to the team.

For the T side, I would say that I have more space now with Finn because his way of calling is a bit more open, so I think he needs players to give more ideas and more directions.

I believe that this is where I can kind of chime in and help him a lot. So in that sense, my role is a bit different now that my voice is a bit more activated in the rounds.

While with Damjan [kyxsan], I was a bit more quiet because he had a different way of calling, which was also fine. And yeah. When it comes to calling, that’s pretty much it.

Then I’m just, I’m a bit more focused on lurking as well because Finn doesn’t like to finish rounds with five people on the one site. So I’m lurking a bit more as well than I used to, and that’s pretty much it.

How are you finding those lurking positions?

I mean, I think it’s been up and down. There are games or events where I feel really good, and then there are some games where I can’t do much for some reason. So I’m still finding myself, I would say.

I think that I have improved my game a little bit since Finn joined. I think I’m a bit more stable, but it has been a difficult transition in these roles, but I’m trying to improve.

I want to be a stable player for the team. I want to, I try to focus on getting better in clutches as well, because this is something that we need on the team. So far, at least this event has been working pretty good and hopefully I can keep it up.

End of interview

Let us know your IEM Cologne 2026 Major predictions on Insider Gaming’s Discord


For other news, see Big John’s participation at Valorant Masters London, bosh! And sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

https://insider-gaming.com/niko-iem-cologne-2026-major-interview/

item.title: Fnatic Sign Cloud to Valorant Roster for VCT EMEA Stage 2
item.content:encoded:
Fnatic has signed experienced initiator Kirill ‘Cloud’ Nekhozhin to its Valorant roster ahead of VCT EMEA Stage 2.

The 22-year-old Russian pro has been the captain and IGL of GIANTX since the organisation joined VCT EMEA in 2023.

Fnatic had to fill a gap in its roster after the departure of Clément ‘CyvOph’ Millard to Natus Vincere.

CyvOph was brought into the Fnatic Valorant team earlier this year after Sylvain ‘Veqaj’ Pattyn had to step down amid health concerns.

However, Fnatic see Cloud as a long-term member of the team, signing him to a three-year contract.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Fnatic’s VCT Woes

After attending every international tournament last year and reaching the Champions finals, 2026 has been difficult for Fnatic.

Fnatic failed to qualify for Valorant Masters London after Team Heretics eliminated them from the Stage 1 Playoffs, denying fan-favourite Jake ‘Boaster’ Howlett the chance to play in front of a home crowd.

This is the first time that Fnatic has failed to qualify for one of the first two internationals in a year.

  • Related: Fnatic CEO claims the organisation isn’t going anywhere despite takeover bids

And on top of that, Fnatic also failed to qualify for the Esports World Cup through the online qualifiers.

Fnatic now have to record a high finish in the VCT EMEA Stage 2 Playoffs in order to qualify for Valorant Champions at the end of the year.

To try and make that happen, Fnatic has also brought in a new head coach, Andrey ‘Engh’ Sholokhov.

While missing players heavily contributed to poor results, Fnatic still decided to part ways with coach Milan ‘Milan’ de Meij.

How good a signing will Cloud be for Fnatic? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And in other news, food-loving influencer Big John is hosting an esports pub at Valorant Masters London Fan Fest.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: LoL Patch 26.13 Buffs Le Blanc and Draven Ahead of MSI 2026
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has released a full preview of League of Legends (LoL) Patch 26.13 with huge changes to the meta.

There is a substantial amount of champion buffs and nerfs in this update, as it will be the patch that LoL MSI 2026 will be played on.

Buffed champions like Draven, Le Blanc and Aphelios could be played a lot at the tournament, especially with multiple Fearless best-of-fives.

Meanwhile, nefs to the likes of Senna, Bard, and K’Sante could knock them down on teams’ priorities.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Phroxzon Explains Buffs and Nerfs

Lead gameplay designer Matt ‘Phroxzon’ Leung-Harrison has given context to Riot’s thinking behind some of these changes.

One champion who is getting substantial nerfs is Senna but Phroxzon claims there are multiple reasons why:

“Senna is the big winner of the Season 2 changes, synergizing well with Doran’s Helm (gets a lot of effective health from her Q heal synergizing with the resists) as well as getting a lot of stats from Jack of All Trades.

“She also synergizes well with Stattik Shiv on-hit, and we’ve made the decision to not support this interaction (Q CD reduction on-hit), even though it is pretty cool.”

  • Related: Senna and Thresh have been added to LoL fighting game 2XKO

He also explained how they’re nerfing Bard, who is doing well in both solo queue and pro play:

“We’re adjusting how much damage his builds do, especially given his tank roaming builds tend to do a lot more damage than expected.”

Meanwhile, Olaf is getting buffs to his jungle clear, but Phroxzon admits they were nervous to give him any more help as he is already doing well in pro play:

“We’re not looking to buff Olaf jungle a huge amount, given that he’s already flex viable in top lane, but given that Jungle was his initial primary role, we want to make it more viable than it currently is.”

LoL Patch 26.13 Balance Changes

Champion Buffs

  • Aphelios
    • Calibrum Mark Damage: 15 (+10% bonus AD) ⇒ 15 (+15% bonus AD)
    • Severum Onslaught Damage: 19-40% Total AD ⇒ 20-41% Total AD
    • Infernum Duskwave Damage: 20-110 (+10-16% bonus AD) ⇒ 20-110 (+15-21% bonus AD)
    • Crescendum Sentry Damage: 35-125 (+30-48% bonus AD) ⇒ 35-125 (+34-52% bonus AD)
  • Draven
    • E CD: 18-14-⇒ 16-12
    • R Mult-ihit Fall off: -8% multiplicative, to a minimum of 40% ⇒ -5% multiplicative, to a minimum of 50%
  • Kai’sa
    • W CD: 22-14 ⇒ 20-14
    • R Base Shield: 70-110 ⇒ 100-200
  • Le Blanc
    • RQ1/RE1: 70/140/210 ⇒ 70/150/230
    • Mark(RQ2)/Root(RE2) damage: 140/280/420 ⇒ 140/300/460
    • RW Damage: 150/300/450 ⇒ 150/315/480
  • Olaf
    • Q Monster bonus physical damage:
    • 20/35/50/65/80 ⇒ 20/45/70/95/120
  • Poppy
    • Q Monster damage cap: 50/80/110/140/170 ⇒ 75/105/135/165/195
  • Qiyana
    • Q Monster damage: 155% ⇒ 175%
  • Vex
    • E CD: 13 ⇒ 12
    • R Takedown Reset Window: 6s ⇒ 8s
  • Zaahen
    • W CD: 16-12 ⇒ 14-12

Champion Nerfs

  • Bard
    • P Meep Damage: 35 (+10 per 5 Chimes) (+40% AP) ⇒ 30 (+6 per 5 Chimes) (+40% AP)
  • Brand
    • Base Mana Regen: 9 per 5 ⇒ 11 per 5
    • E Mana Cost: 70-90 ⇒ 90
    • P Detonation Damage: 8-12% (+2% per 100 AP) Max Health ⇒ 6-12% (+2% per 100 AP) Max Health
  • Cassiopeia
    • Health per level: 104 ⇒ 98
  • K’Sante
    • Shield (80-240) (+15% bonus health) ⇒ (70-240) (+13.5% bonus health)
  • Rek’Sai
    • E physical damage:
      • 80/108/136/164/192 (+64% bonus AD) ⇒ 70/95/120/145/170 (+60% bonus AD)
      • bonus true damage: 125% ⇒ 120%
  • Rumble
    • Q Damage: 60-160 (+100%AP) ⇒ 50-150 (+105%AP)
  • Senna
    • Minion/Small Monster Soul Drop Chance: 10% when Senna kills / 28% when Senna doesn’t kill ⇒ 5% when
    • Senna kills / 32% when Senna doesn’t kill
    • Crit Damage Reduction: -10% ⇒ -15% (ex: 170% +25.5% IE)
    • Q Cooldown Refund: 1s On Hit ⇒ 1s On Attack
  • Sion
    • W Mana: 65-85 ⇒ 75-95
    • Q Min Damage: 40-120 ⇒ 30-90 (max damage unchanged)

System Buffs

  • Imperial Mandate
    • Build Path: Blasting Wand + Bandleglass + 650g ⇒ 2xAmp Tome + Bandleglass + 700g
    • AP: 65 ⇒ 60
    • Unique Passive – Control: +15AH on Immobilizing Abilities ⇒ +20AH on Immobilizing Abilities
    • Unique Passive – Command: 6% Vulnerable on Immobilizing ⇒ 7% Vulnerable on Immobilizing

System Nerfs

  • Doran’s Helm
    • Health: 140 ⇒ 150
    • Armor and Magic Resist: 10 ⇒ 8

Which of these changes do you expect to have the biggest impact on the meta? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, legendary LoL toplaner TheShy has returned to Invictus Gaming.

MOBILE APP
